title: Go-nogo categorization and detection task
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from participants performing go-nogo categorization and detection tasks across two recording sessions. Participants viewed briefly presented images (20 ms) and responded according to task-specific rules: lifting a finger for target stimuli (go) or maintaining button press for non-targets (nogo) in an animal categorization task, or recognizing learned probe images in a recognition task. Participants were given 1000 ms to respond, after which any response was considered a nogo response. The dataset includes 25 series of 100 images each across two days, with high temporal resolution motor response measurements (sub-millisecond latency).
methods_description: EEG recordings were conducted with participants seated 110 cm from a computer screen in a dimly lit room. Visual stimuli (8-bit color photographs, 256×384 pixels) were presented for 20 ms using a programmable graphics board at 100 Hz refresh rate. Participants responded via a touch-sensitive button with sub-millisecond response latency measurement. Stimulus onset asynchrony was 2000 ms ± 200 ms random delay. Two tasks alternated: animal categorization (respond to animal images) and recognition (identify learned probe images). Testing occurred across two days with 13 series on day 1 and 12 series on day 2.
